title: GetResponse Review 2025: Features, Pricing & Honest Verdict
meta_description: Is GetResponse worth it in 2025? We break down pricing, features, pros and cons so you can decide.
h1: GetResponse Review 2025: Everything You Need to Know
intro: GetResponse is complete online marketing platform that positions itself for online course creators and webinar hosts. Founded in 1998, it has become one of the most recognised platforms in the email marketing space. In this review we cover what it does well, where it falls short, and who should actually use it.
verdict: GetResponse is a solid choice for online course creators and webinar hosts. Its free tier is genuinely useful. The main trade-off is between its particular strengths and the limitations that make alternatives worth considering for some users.
pros: Good fit for online course creators and webinar hosts
cons: Can get expensive at higher subscriber counts
Some features locked to higher tiers
pricing_summary: GetResponse offers a free plan, with paid plans from $19/month. Pricing typically scales with subscriber count.
best_for_detail: GetResponse works best for online course creators and webinar hosts. If you fit that profile, the feature set aligns well. If you need something different, check the alternatives section.

faq.a: Yes, GetResponse has a free plan supporting up to 500 subscribers. This makes it accessible for new senders testing the platform.

faq.q: What is GetResponse best used for?
faq.a: GetResponse is best for online course creators and webinar hosts. Its standout features include email marketing, webinars, landing pages, which suit this use case well.
GetResponse is generally beginner-friendly. The interface is straightforward for basic campaigns, though advanced automation features have a learning curve.

faq.q: Does GetResponse have an affiliate program?
faq.a: Yes, GetResponse runs an affiliate program paying 33% recurring or $100 one-time. This is worth considering if you plan to recommend the platform to your audience.
